headline: What Exxon Mobil (XOM)'s Board Shift and Supply Rerouting Amid Gulf Tensions Means For Shareholders
summary: In February 2026, Exxon Mobil announced that director Jeffrey W. Ubben would not stand for re‑election at the May 27 annual meeting, while the company also began arranging gasoline and fuel shipments from the US Gulf Coast to Australia as Strait of Hormuz traffic was severely disrupted by the US‑Iran conflict. These moves highlight how Exxon Mobil’s governance changes and flexible global supply chains intersect with rising geopolitical risk and shifting oil flows tied to Middle East...
